entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

As follow-up to our previous release 2408, this blog post provides an update concerning our most recent release 2411, which is available for customers and partners since November 16th. This particular release features highlights such as ABAP Cloud in SAP Build, a new approach to build Application Jobs, and the region-less subscription of the Landscape Portal.

The complete list of all new features can be found on the What´s New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment page.

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Creating an ABAP Cloud Project in the Context of the SAP BTP ABAP environment, a service key is no longer needed when creating an ABAP Cloud Project. You now enter the ABAP Service instance URL (details)
  • Triggering Where-Used List for Usages of CDS Annotation to enable the investigation in which ABAP CDS an annotation is used
  • Displaying Field Characteristics for RAP Types in the Element Information Popup, The Element Information popup (F2) for RAP types shows additional field characteristics information such as mandatory:create, mandatory:execute or readonly (details)
  • Multi-Dimensional Analysis App Generator, you can now use the multi-dimensional analysis app generator wizard to create all ABAP development objects that are needed to build, expose, and deploy a multi-dimensional analysis Fiori app (details)
  • Support of SAP Fiori launchpad app descriptor items in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, these items describe how an application is started and consist of a target mapping and one or more tiles, for more information see SAP Fiori Applications in the ABAP Environment and Working with Launchpad App Descriptor Items
  • Rerunning Unit Tests with Coverage in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • Clearing Coverage results, you can now clear your coverage results in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • ABAP Test Cockpit: ATC Check "Usage of Released APIs" can be run on Key User and ABAP Cloud objects

Reuse Services

  • New Class Interface for Application Job Catalog Entries and Templates, with the new attribute-based class interface it is now possible to change the parameters, sections, and groups of a catalog entry (details)
  • Business Rule Framework (BRF plus) is enhanced with a code generation service and an API introduced to facilitate the consumption of business rules within ABAP Cloud (details)
  • XCO XSLX: You can now style the cells of an Excel worksheet. You can also modify existing documents (details)
  • XCO CDS: You can now generate CDS external entities and logical external schemas, and you can now add comments and arithmetic expressions to CDS views (details)

UI Services and SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Creating Launchpad App Descriptor Items (details)

Integration

  • With Business Event Logging, you can capture and log business events locally within the ABAP environment
  • App Display Business Event Logs. It provides an overview of logged business events for different objects and event types. You can explore the details of the event with this app
  • App Display Business Events by Objects: It groups business events by instances of objects and object components
  • App Display Changes to Objects: You can see the changes for your objects on a field level as recorded in business event logging

Security

  • ABAP environment tenants are now equipped with a signed default client certificate, which is automatically rotated and can be used for customer managed integration scenarios
  • The communication scenario SAP_COM_0915 provides access to APIs to read the Read Access Logs from the system, the API can for instance be leveraged by a Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) system
  • TLS 1.3, we now support Transport Layer Security 1.3 for outbound HTTP, WebSocket RFC, and SMTP protocols
  • The system exposes APIs to retrieve customer-managed security settings in a read-only manner

Custom Code Migration App

  • Include ADT links in SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check Export, the export of ATC results for the SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check now includes HTTP(S) links, which enables you to navigate to the source code of example code in ADT for Eclipse. This new functionality is available in the Custom Code Migration app only (details)
  • New option to start analysis after project creation, you can now choose whether you would like to start the custom code analysis run automatically at project creation or whether you would like to schedule it manually after project creation (details)
  • Changes in Scope Calculation Automation, you now need to initiate scope calculation manually after project creation, the scope calculation no longer starts automatically at project creation (details)

Cloud ATC

  • Change Validity Date for ATC Exemptions in the Approve ATC Exemptions App (details)
  • Replication of ATC Exemptions from on-premise towards SAP BTP, Customers need to use transaction SATC_EXPORT_XMPT to download the exemptions from an on-premise ATC system (details)

Technical Monitoring

  • Enhanced app HANA Table Analysis: With the new Column Loads and Column Unloads screens in the HANA Table Analysis app, you can check how many columns of the tables in your HANA database have been loaded to or unloaded from the main storage (details)
  • New app HANA Memory Analysis: With the new app, you can get an overview of the memory consumption of the HANA memory components over time. This helps you to identify bottlenecks in your HANA memory (details)

Lifecycle Management

  • When cloning a software component, you can now optionally use the newly added parameters to request cloning a Bring Your Own Git (BYOG) repository (details)

abapGit

  • Support of object deletions, it can now be exported from and imported to the ABAP environment with abapGit (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Extended Access, you can now access and subscribe to the Landscape Portal for SAP BTP ABAP Environment from every region that is available for the ABAP environment. In regions without SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service, some applications in the Landscape Portal are limited or not available (details)
  • Deinstalling Products, the new Undeploy Product app in the Landscape Portal enables you to permanently deinstall existing products (details)

entry.title: #07 - Exploring Your SAP Solution: Engaging Stakeholders Early in the Implementation Process
entry.content:
Why is it important to familiarize yourself with your solution prior to implementation?

Let me share a story about a customer I worked with who took the time to enable their team before starting the project implementation and design discussions.

This customer was new to SAP and purchased a solution that was unfamiliar to them, yet critical for their business. As we recommend to all our customers, we advised them to undergo solution enablement before project implementation. This approach would help them become more familiar with the solution and maximize its value throughout the implementation process.

You’re probably wondering how it went for that customer. Well, I can tell you—it went really well!

When we began the design workshops, we presented the standard capabilities, as we always recommend to our customers for creating a scalable solution. However, we also recognize that business-specific requirements often call for adjustments. During these discussions, our customer was not only better prepared to engage with the topics and explanations we provided, but they were also able to identify key functionalities that would help enhance their business.

These design discussions are crucial during project implementation because they define the foundation of the customer’s solution. If the design is not done correctly from the start, the customer may end up with a solution that isn’t viable. Moreover, having the right solution design in place ensures that, during the testing phase, you're validating the solution effectively—rather than testing irrelevant processes that don’t align with the business needs. And that’s exactly what happened—my customer ended up with a solution that perfectly met their needs.

Another benefit was the time efficiency. By enabling themselves early on, the workshop discussions ran smoothly and stayed within the defined timelines, allowing us to progress without any delays. And it wasn’t just in the early stages where we were time-efficient; throughout all phases of the project implementation, we stayed on track because the scope of what needed to be delivered was clear from the start.

Finally, the entire team was engaged from day one and understood the impact of implementing a new solution. Typically, when a new solution is being implemented, not all users immediately embrace the change. Senior leadership is eager to move to a new system to improve business performance, increase margins and save costs, but business users are often resistant, especially when they are unfamiliar with the new system.

However, by enabling key stakeholders early on, we were able to make a significant impact on the skeptical business users. These stakeholders were able to champion the solution, sharing the added value it brought for the business users by streamlining their daily tasks. This empowered the business users to engage during the discussions and focus on higher-value activities, keeping them motivate. It also reinforced that everyone was contributing to the overall success of the business.

 

In a nutshell:

Gaining knowledge about SAP solution ahead of time is crucial, allows customers to maximize the value of the system from day one. 

By familiarizing themselves with the features, functionalities, and best practices before implementation, customers can ensure a smoother transition, reduce potential challenges, and align the solution more effectively with their business goals. This proactive approach allows teams to ask informed questions, make strategic decisions, and prepare their organization for a more seamless integration.

Furthermore, early exposure to the SAP solution empowers customers to take full advantage of its capabilities, ensuring they are ready to leverage its tools and insights to drive business success once the system goes live.

Stay curious and never stop learning!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Ana Barreira serves as the Global Onboarding Advisor for the SAP Transportation Management (TM) solution within the Supply Chain Management area. She provides expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature through to successful go-live. With eleven years of experience—three of which have been as a Global Onboarding Advisor, and eight years as an SAP Consultant—Ana specializes in the SAP TM solution. She has successfully supported full-cycle customer implementation projects, assisted partners, and contributed to internal enablement efforts

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ana_Barreira  or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Output Management Solutions For LOBs In SAP Business Technology Platform To Keep Core Clean
entry.content:
In our LOBs systems like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA OnPremise, scope item Output Management (1LQ) will cover the requirements of rendering PDF form used by printing, email.
 
But there are scenarios where the requirements can not be realized with standard function of 1LQ,the following are some examples:
  • Missing data even tried with key user extensibility.
  • Missing functions like need digital signature in PDF form.
  • Customers need pull data from multiple systems to render PDF form in realtime.
  • Customers need a stand alone application for partners to print PDF form with data from LOB systems in realtime.
  • Customer need to render PDF form in mobile device with data from LOB systems in realtime.
  • Partners want to deploy SAAS solution for customers for Output Management.
  • Other scenarios.
Some scenarios can be realized with On-stack extensibility in ABAP environment. To keep core clean,cloud ABAP is recommended, classic technologies like smartform, SAPscript should be avoided. Adobe Forms technology is recommended.
 
To keep core clean, Side-By-Side extensibility in SAP Business Technology Platform(BTP) are highly recommended for loosely-coupled scenarios. For Output Management solutions in BTP, we have 2 styles solutions.
 
First style is automatically label printing or email. This means when business object like billing created in LOB system, the PDF form is automatically rendered with predefined format are automatically sent to a local printer or emailed to specific contact. I wrote a blog Build Digital Document Platform Backend Service On BTP with BTP Ads Sdm Print Service before. If we use the solution in this blog, we can realize automatically label printing with SAP Integration Suite or SAP Build Process Automation very efficiently. The following is a solution architecture for a real customer use case.

 
Second style is Side-By-Side extensibility with CAP or RAP as the backend, SAP UI5 as the front end.
Here will deploy a SAP UI5 application in SAP Build Work Zone or SAP BTP ABAP environment for the end users. The following is one of the architectures:

 

For this style, we prepared the following Discovery Center Missions for you:
  • If you prefer CAP in NodeJs, you can follow the mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js
  • If you prefer CAP in Java,you can follow the mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Java
  •  If you prefer RAP(Cloud ABAP),you can follow the mission Add Printing Label to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud In BTP ABAP Environment
All the 3 missions are base on best practice of Side-By-Side extensibility. You can apply the same method in other Side-By-Side extensibility scenarios.
 
The mission Add Printing Label to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud is based on a little old technology. I will retire the mission.
 
To better help our customers to learn the solutions, we prepared the following webinars in December 2024. Welcome to register with the following linkages, we are happy to explain and answer queries and discuss.
 
Little remind: please reach out to your BTP Customer Success Manager to get the CRM ID for your organisation before the register.
  • 4TH December 2024, Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js
  • 11ST December 2024, Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Java
These 2 solutions are based on CAP. If you are interested in CAP,I think you can start with the Learning Sources.



The Side-By-Side extensibility method used in the missions can be applied to other LOBs like SAP ECC,SAP SuccessFactors HCM,etc.

 

The ends!
Thanks for your time!

 

Best regards!
Jacky Liu

entry.title: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP

    🧰👷‍Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects👷‍🧰

 

 

Having found this "RISE & BTP 1 Pager for Technical Architects", if you're interested in this subject, then subscribe to the SAP Innovation and Transformation newsletter, here's the link:  Innovation & Transformation Newsletter SAP Services and Support

RISE with SAP (S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on SAP RISE) is re-writing the book on how companies "do" SAP, and infact, everybody on that journey or planning for or preparing for that journey is re-writing the book.

There are so many myths and misunderstandings about RISE, hopefully this work will shine more light on the subject.

The goal of this work is to provide a SAP RISE "1 Pager" for all of us Customer/System Integrator side folks who are on this journey. 

And back in 2013 when I was studying for SAP HANA Certification I published the following blog, as a central point of reference for SAP HANA technical information, it helped me with what I was studying and helped others with having piles of very useful information in one place:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/the-sap-hana-reference-for-sap-basis-administrators/ba-p/13007539

Fast forward 12 years, and we are now in the same situation with SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP, there is useful documentation all over the place and it would help if there was a central location for SAP Technology Architects to use as a "Toolbox". It will certainly help me 🙂

Implementing and Migrating to and Running S/4 PCE on RISE for the last three years, and as usual collecting a mountain of the most useful documentation as a personal toolbox, it's been clear to see that similar to the situation back in the earlier period of HANA, there is useful RISE documentation in a number of locations, this blog will act as a central resource pointing to all of the locations of the SAP S/4 PCE RISE documentation.

And similarly for the BTP, having been doing BTP (or as it was the SAP Cloud Platform) for 7 years, this blog will be a central location pointing to the most useful BTP documentation for SAP Technology Architects.

The two subjects are combined, SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE with BTP, because in my eyes the two go hand in hand, S/4HANA PCE on RISE is the next generation of the previous On-Premise ERP offerings from SAP, and the BTP contains the next generation of Applications which we will all use with the S/4HANA PCE on RISE, including for example, and not limited to, Integration Suite and CPI & EM/AEM & APIM, obviously CALM as this is replacing in many but not all cases what we do with SolMan, and all of the other Services which we will need pretty much day by day to run S/4HANA PCE on RISE, and of course, Steampunk, keeping the Core Clean, and "Upgrade Stable" (as my friend Sakthivel Sivaguru would say), then we need the ABAP Service in the BTP for the side car extensions. But that's just the BTP "Starter Pack", as you go on the BTP journey you will be using all kinds of Services and including the AI Joule and the Business Application Studio and the Work Zone, Custom Domain, ADS, Secure Logon, CTMS  etc and then of course the Intelligent Services across the Business Processes, eg Intelligent Account Reconciliation, there's a link to an Excel sheet here, which lists all of the Business Processes and how they are Intelligently enabled by SAP Technologies.

If you have links to useful documentation which you think will help others and should be added to the list, then please just write it in the comments and I will add it to the list.

For 25 years I have been hunting and collecting SAP documentation, my library spans the whole journey from before the New Dimension Products, through NetWeaver 2004, through HANA DB, through S/4HANA, through SAP Cloud Platform and on through the current phase S/4HANA Cloud aka "Grow with SAP", S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition aka "RISE with SAP", and the Business Technology Platform and the Intelligent Technologies. Hunting a collecting documentation is a useful skill, and here's a tip from my friend Jarod Sandham - some useful search criteria to find documentation is to search "https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/ <insert search criteria here>, eg open google and search for:  https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/  security

Please expect to find changes and new information here, because it will be a live work in progress, it's going to take a few weeks to list and classify everything that I already have. Plans for immediate future are for each link to add a screenshot of the most useful information behind that link, and that's going to take a few weeks, and at the same time the list will just get longer.

And finally the ask to SAP... if friends from SAP are reading this work, and know about new versions of documents/materials or new documents/materials which are available either publicly or with an S User and could enrich this work, please let me know either directly or in the chat and I will incorporate that material to the benefit of everyone.

Disclaimer: For the record, regarding the pictures and screenshots, ALL pictures are from documents which are publicly available and searchable on google, there are no pictures from any SAP documentation which is protected behind an authentication mechanism. There are many links here to documents where you will need to have an S User to be able to read them, and there are no screenshots from any of those documents. Every screenshot/picture contains a statement that this document is publicly available on the Internet.

This is the journey you are going on...

 

Source: 20230328_sap_dmlt.pdf

 

Source:  20230328_sap_dmlt.pdf

 

 

Ok, let's go 🚀

 

 

Contents:

Section 1] General Introduction Documents and RISE Getting Started Guides

Section 1.01] The SAP & Customer Shared Responsibilities on RISE

Section 1.02] The S/4HANA 3 Transition & Migration & Implementation Options

Section 1.1] Implementing and Running S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on SAP RISE

Section 2] SAP ECS Enterprise Cloud Services

Section 2.1] Azure Useful Links

Section 2.2] AWS Amazon Web Services Useful Links

Section 3] Security on SAP RISE and ECS 

Section 4] Data Migration and Transition

Section 5] SAP BTP General

Section 5.1] SAP CALM Cloud Application LifeCycle Management

Section 5.1.1] Monitoring with SAP CALM - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 5.1.2] SAP Build Work Zone

Section 5.2] Extending S/4HANA on RISE - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 5.3] Integrating S/4HANA on RISE - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 6] SAP Customer Center of Excellence

Section 7] Lessons Learned

_________________________________________________________________

 

Section 1] General Introduction Documents and RISE Getting Started Guides

What is RISE from the viewpoint of a SAP Technology Architect ?  

The following picture is from the publicly available document Presentation Title

 

This one below is from here:  PowerPoint Presentation

 

 

 

 

How do you do RISE ?

Regardless of what anybody thinks about all of the different methodologies, every SAP Customer who is contemplating the move to S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on RISE must begin by reading about the "RISE with SAP Methodology".

This is basically a guided procedure including supporting documentation to help SAP Customers get to S/4HANA in the smoothest and straightest line possible. 

Naturally SAP have a recommended tool chain to support for following the SAP RISE Methodology, and regardless of whether on not you use SAP's recommended tools, this picture is very useful because it elaborates a little more what is happening at each part of the journey:

Here's an introduction to the methodology RISE with SAP | Methodology

This is the overview of the guided procedure:

This picture comes from this document which is publicly available: Mapping your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition : A practical guide for senior IT leadership

These are the core tasks of the methodology:

 

day_1_session_2b_pnp_application_modernization_migration.pdf

I recommend everybody who will be part of the journey to read that document as one of the first steps, once everybody has read that document, everybody will be on the same page as to what lies ahead.

Here are more documents which are very useful during the preparation:

SAP Activate Methodology for SAP S/4HANA Transformation

https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/AAE80671-5087-430B-9AA7-8FBE881CF548/phaseAccelerator/S4HANATRANSONPRE

RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition Licensing Overview

https://me.sap.com/readinesscheck/home

RISE OnBoarding Resource Center

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise/rise-private.html

RISE OnBoarding Handbook Page

The Handbook is another MUST READ and is publicly available and contains a huge amount of useful practical information, like this, what happens after signing the SAP RISE Contract:

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise.html

 

Then you are reminded of the overview of who does what...

 

 

and then you are reminded that to be a Future Ready Enterprise you are going to need a BTP...

 

 

Source:  https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/webinars/sap-user-groups-k4u/pdfs/differentiating_your_core_erp_with_sap_s4hana_240227.pdf

And there is a link to an excellent publicly available document on the Future Ready Enterprise with the BTP FRE Best Practice Guide for RISE Transformation_Private Cloud.pdf

BTP is covered in detail in the BTP section of the Toolbox

 

This is a really useful document, it walks you through a theoretical S/4HANA Transformation and including all of the steps Transformation Plan 1.0 - Outcome summary of SAP S/4HANA Adoption Starter

Moving to RISE Differentiating your Core ERP with S/4HANA PCE

Presentation Title

RISE with SAP Transition Workbench

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/940e4b48bd434bcb95db32940d08694f/cc75825f2d7049db96b6c60ec269aac6.html

Activate Roadmap Viewer:

https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/

Click on

"RISE with SAP Clean Core Success Plan" and it takes you to

"SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition"

https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContentPage/a11ec6d657054bf9:t23

Clean Core will get it's own section in the 1 Pager, because it is such an important subject, in the mean time, there are a number of links in here regarding Clean Core, just search for them, and as a taster on the subject, this picture from this excellent document gives a nice explanation as to what Clean Core is about and why we should have it as a our goal, and as a minimum to be upgrade stable so that we can benefit from the innovations which we are paying for in the license... https://www.sap.com/documents/2019/05/44b3ebd5-4b7d-0010-87a3-c30de2ffd8ff.html

Section 1.01] The SAP & Customer Shared Responsibilities on RISE

This section is exceptionally important, from the SAP Basis and Operations perspective there are naturally a lot of questions around, how does the Basis function work on RISE. What does SAP do, what does the Customer (and their SI) do ? There are a lot of misunderstandings and incorrect myths.

The goal of this section is to help the SAP Customer who is thinking about RISE to build a cleaer picture and understanding of what the responsibilities are and what the segregation of duties are.

A lot of people seem to think that on RISE the Customer won't need a Basis Team, well, far from it, on RISE the Customer obviously won't need so many Basis people, but, and the big but is, the Customer will need exceptionally qualified and experienced Basis people who at the same time have a very good maturity and discipline in their behaviour. One of my biggest lessons, and there are many many learnings, has been, with RISE, due to the nature of the shared responsibilities, there is very little room for trial and error in the Basis Domain, and the Basis Team need to put massive effort in to proper planning and preparation of activities and understand who will do what bases upon the segregation of duties between SAP and the Customer. There is no room for trial and error when doing new things in the Basis area.

So, we could write a book about that, but we are here to share useful documentation, so here it comes...

RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition Security and Compliance

Don't be deceived by the title, this document contains some golden information, anybody wanting to understand who does what with RISE, here are pictures you need, these picture show where your responsibilities are:

We start with a higher level view which is from the RISE OnBoarding Handbook Onboarding Handbook .pdf

In my opinion the above picture is not detailed enough, it doesn't communicated really how much responsibility the Customer SAP Basis Team will have. (The detailed list of ECS Services is here: SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition - Service Specifications)

The following pictures go into some more detail on the Customer's SAP Basis responsibilities:

The above pictures are from the publicly available document Check & ProductivityPlus Training

RISE with SAP: Shared Security Responsibility for SAP Cloud Services

There are some great pictures and explanations in this blog, like this one:

Section 1.02] The S/4HANA 3 Transition & Migration & Implementation Options

There are three ways to get to S/4HANA PCE on RISE:

Greenfield - new clean S/4HANA

Bluefield Selective Data Transition - search this page for Bluefield and you will find the latest guides on how to do this

Brownfield Transformation - transform and convert an existing ECC in to an S/4HANA

This document is publicly available: 20230328_sap_dmlt.pdf

I'm not going to write much more on this in my own words because there is so much very useful documentation available from SAP on this subject.

This section of the Toolbox will provide links to lots of useful supporting documents and highlight very useful diagrams which will help for understanding your situation and your options.

It's less about tossing a coin and more about understanding what you want to achieve and where you want to get to. The following documents and pictures will explain why...

Please note some of these documents are older and some are newer, the older ones are using older terminology, but the essentials are the same and that's why they are included here, because the message they communicate is useful

There is not too much information in the public domain on what the SAP ECS Team is doing, they are your Partner for pretty much everything, they are like your Cloud/Unix/Technical-Layer-Basis wrapped in to one, the majority of the really useful documentation on what the ECS Team do and how to work with them is listed below, but, you will need an S User and in some cases need to be a RISE Customer to access the documentation.

This section of the Toolbox is really aimed at Basis people and Unix Team / Cloud Hosting / Network people from the Customer side, and so, I am including here the most useful pictures from documents in the public domain to help with introductions as to how the networking to SAP ECS is working, how the hosting is working and how the security of it all is working.

Section 5] SAP BTP General

SAP Business Technology Platform BTP is a massive Platform, and in my opinion will be bigger and more important than the S/4HANA for SAP Customers in the future. The BTP is a technology enabler containing all kinds of capabilities.

The first thing I am going to comment on, since this is a 1 Pager for SAP Technology Architects is the Technical Architecture of the BTP.

The BTP is extremely rich and flexible in how it can be set up and configured, and this can be a good thing and a bad thing, the biggest challenge is, making foundation layer mistakes which cost time and money to correct in the future.

For me to try show how complex the BTP is, we will first look at SAP's Future Ready Enterprise Framework, this is an approach to modularising maturity with the BTP and leading the Customer on a journey to BTP Architecture Excellence.

You've heard of a SAP Customer Center of Excellence, there is now a concept of SAP BTP Center of Excellence, this shows how complex and rich this subject is.

There is a beautiful publicly available presentation here  on the Future Ready Enterprise and the SAP BTP Future Ready Enterprise Unlock and Maximize the value of BTP

With wonderful slides like these...

 

 

 

You don't necessarily need to engage SAP's Services on this, at least by studying and digesting the picture you can think about how those dimensions apply to your Organisation and fill in the boxes in terms of the objectives and vision of your Organisation

 

 

 

These slides are a humbling reminder of how vast the BTP is, and how getting a decent maturity level in the BTP will help your Organisation achieve its goals

 

 

So now we know, the BTP is big, and it's complex, and it needs proper planning and architecting if we are going to get the most from it.

For  the first few years the SAP Cloud Platform was mainly SAP Cloud Platform Integration and a lot of people slipped in to a bad habit of associating the BTP with Integration capability, we it's a lot more than that and we all need to appreciate that.

The following document is another useful introduction to the SAP Future Ready Enterprise Framework:

Presentation Title

The following picture is here to show us just how complex the BTP is, and let us all reflect on where our Company's maturity level is with the BTP:

 

 

Just spend some time to study that table and think about where your Organisation's maturity level is and whether or not it would be a good investment to buy some BTP Training and Services from SAP. I am not trying to sell Services here, but I have learned the hard way that an investment in knowledge can save significant amounts in the future.

This picture which is from the same deck is to really show us what a journey BTP maturity is...

 

There is a very nice blog by @Pedro_Souza  on the same subject here The Future-Ready Enterprise: A Framework for SAP B... - SAP Community

The next step for the SAP Technology Architect is to read the BTP Admin Guide SAP BTP Administrator's Guide | SAP Help Portal  and read the section on SetUp and Plan Set Up and Plan | SAP Help Portal the whole section including the Governance Model. The BTP Admin Guide as a pdf..  SAP_Cloud_Platform.pdf

SAP BTP Platform Configuration Guide 

PlatformConfiguration.pdf

SAP BTP Common Concepts and Strategic Directio

Presentation Title

SAP BTP Data Management in a Nutshell

Title Goes Here and Here and Here

Then read the section Setting up your Account Model Setting Up Your Account Model | SAP Help Portal

The biggest lesson I can bring is, when architecting the BTP from a clean sheet of paper, one of the most important foundation steps is to correctly design the Stages / Directories / Sub-Accounts and Entitlements, the setting up of the Account Model, get this wrong and you will pay for it later.

BTP Domains / Stages / Directories / Sub-Accounts

Domain refers to the Capability, eg Cloud Integration, Work Zone, Build Process Automation

Stages refers to the type of environment, eg Dev, Test, Quality, UAT, Prod

Directory refers to the structuring elements for Sub-Accounts

Sub-Account let's you further structure according to your requirements

My recomendation is go for an Account Model with Directories and Sub-Accounts Account Model With Directories and Subaccounts | SAP Help Portal

The reason for this is:

You then have the possibility for all kinds of template BTP layouts according to the demands of your Organisation and at the same time you can leverage all of the benefits described above

 

 

 

And then as already described you profit from being able to leverage Authorisation and Entitlement features per Directory / BTP Domain

Setting Up Your Account Model | SAP Help Portal

To recap setting up the Account Model is probably the most important BTP Technical Architecture decision !

This is the Future Ready Enterprise document which is referred to in the RISE Onboarding Handbook FRE Best Practice Guide for RISE Transformation_Private Cloud.pdf

Next step go to the SAP Discovery Center and look at the BTP OnBoarding material SAP Discovery Center Mission Public Card - Live and OnDemand Webinars about SAP BTP Onboarding

 

And then go to the SAP BTP OnBoarding center SAP Business Technology Platform General Concepts

After doing all of this preparation, the next step is to Architect and Administer your BTP, and for that the next step is to read the SAP BTP Administrator's Guide SAP BTP Administrator's Guide | SAP Help Portal

 

For SAP Build and all of the Services around it have a look at this presentation

2023_sap_build_webinar_april_4.pdf

Section 5.3] AI & Intelligent Technologies

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-generative-ai.html

Intelligent Technologies and Business Processes Excel Sheet

https://d.dam.sap.com/a/hHzXdEW?rc=10

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3393699

 

 

Section 6] SAP Customer Center of Excellence

(This section is a work in progress)

You might be asking yourself why in a SAP RISE Toolbox there are links to how to do a SAP CCOE ?

That's a great question, and the answer is that, most companies have been running/operating SAP the same way since 3.1h, and now with RISE the whole game is changing, the Roles and Responsibilities are changing and the Segregation of Duties is changing, between who does what, SAP, Customer, and if they have one the Customer's System Integrator. 

And that's why, during the S/4HANA RISE Transformation, you will be best off, if you take the opportunity and redesign how you run/operate SAP, the way you have run SAP for the ECC generation will not fit to how you run SAP in the RISE generation, and on top of this, isn't it time for "The Business" and "IT/SAP" to get closer together ?

SAP offer a fantastic service for helping Customers on this journey, S/4HANA & RISE is not only an ERP Transformation, it's also an Organisational Transformation, and you should look at the SAP North Start Architecture Services, in my 25 years of doing SAP these are the most valuable Services I have seen as North Star Architectures brings the knowledge of all of SAP's Customers and Best Practices on how to run SAP. 

https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/11/c272ac9a-9b7e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

 

The SAP Customer CoE Journey:

Where are you today ? And where do you want to be ?

The above pictures are publicly available and come from this document: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_strategy_framework.pdf

Guides and Best Practices for Customer COE

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html

SAP Customer CoE Strategy, Governance, and Organisation

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_strategy_framework.pdf

Landscape Management Process

https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/landscape-management-process.html?anchorId=section_175842419

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/business-transformation-requires-organizational-transformation/ba-p/13511386

CCOE Media Library

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/media-library.html

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discover-organizational-change-management-for-sap-cloud-projects/how-is-sap-s-organizational-change-management-framework-structured_c1708e6a-28f8-4245-8392-d83cbf48773b

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discover-organizational-change-management-for-sap-cloud-projects

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html?anchorId=section

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe-hints.pdf

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe.html?anchorId=section_541877121_co

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/applying-the-sap-enterprise-architecture-framework

This is really useful

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-as-service-provider/primary-ccoe/CCOE_GettingStarted.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_strategy_framework.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_licenses_framework.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_information_knowledge.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_supportability_en.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_continuous_success_en.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/media-library/general-information/continuous-improvement-and-innovation-with-a-ccoe.pdf

 

 

Section 7] Lessons Learned

(this section is an ongoing work in progress)

Don't skip "Phase 0", plan to give Phase Zero the time it needs

Key Users

Key Users are one of the keys to a successful GoLive make sure they are trained and have all of the support they need

 
Key Users are your Ambassadors, make sure they have everything they need
 
Investment and Multi-Year Multiple Release/GoLive Transformations
 
Everything is relative, whatever you can afford, make sure to invest in the success of the first GoLive/Release,do not cut costs or corners on the first GoLive/Release, the first GoLive/Release MUST be a SUCCESS.
 
Subsequent GoLives/Releases will in general be a rinse and repeat of the template for how
the first GoLive/Release was executed and you can reduce costs on those GoLives/Releases
 
S/4 Data Transformation and Integration
 
We need to do Mapping and Transformations of Data, either at the SAP End or the other End - this is another challenge to the "Clean-Core" and the S/4 Transformations, there needs to be a principle, mappings/transformation must happen outside of the S/4
 
Technical GoLive should be separated / disconnected from the Business GoLive
 
Technical GoLive several months before Business GoLive

RISE Proactive Maintenance for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Contractual Maintenance Periods

SAP and Customer agree regular periods where there can be downtimes for SAP to do Maintenance

Plan this in your Release Planning

S/4HANA Upgrades

In your multi-year Release Plan, make room for S/4HANA Upgrades to enable you to benefit from the latest SAP innovations

Capacity

You need to care for your Capacity and Data Volume Management

Basis Projects and Operations

You need to re-design your Project Basis Team and Operations Basis Team and methods of working according to the segregation of duties with the RISE ECS Team

Run SGEN straight after systems are delivered and before GoLives

Performance Tuning & House Keeping  - that's your job

FUE Licensing Costs and SAP Roles and Authorisations

SAP RISE S/4HANA usage is measured and sized in what’s called FUE’s, Full Usage Equivalents​, pay attention to this

SAP RISE AntiVirus

VSI is a extra service, not mandatory as of today

EWA Report

Pay very close to attention to it, it will become one of your best friends

S/4HANA FailOver and DR

Understand what you really get with 99.7 and 99.9 regarding Availability Zones and Regions

S/4HANA Preparation

Before installation, specify all Security Requirements and get them included in the installation and post installation

RISE is about Segregation of Duties on the Basis Team

RISE is about running Basis as a Factory with clear Roles and Responsibilities and Methods or Working

RISE is about Proper Planning an Preparation

Basis Trial and Error way of working evolves to Planning and Preparation way of working

 
Service Requests
 
Quality
 
Right First Time
 
SAP do not quality control Service Requests
 
Learn the Templates
 
RISE Technical Architecture
 
Is not the same as OnPremise
 
A RISE LoadBalancer or Application Gateway, does not necessarily work the same way on RISE as it does in your own DataCenter/HyperScaler even if they are the same
 
The SAP RISE Architecture View
 
Get from SAP as much technical architecture views of the RISE "Customer Setup" as possible
 
SAP Technical Architects are the an integral part of the new Basis Team
 
Basis Team benefits from being experienced and disciplined 
 
RISE Architect from SAP
 
Get a SAP RISE Architect from SAP onboarded to the project from the beginning

SAP CALM is your new best friend

Sizing and Performance Testing better in than out

Trust and Verify

There are very useful RISE lessons learned here from @shaji_narayanan 

Lessons Learned from SAP ERP Conversion to RISE wi... - SAP Community

 

And most of all, enjoy the RISE Journey 🛫

 

Andy Silvey.

SAP Technology Architect [Contractor]

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

entry.title: From Application Logging to Cloud Logging: Service Migration Guide
entry.content:
This service migration guide provides a 6-step procedure with which you can smoothly setup, test, and eventually handover logging from Application Logging to Cloud Logging service.

It offers a side-by-side setup of Cloud Logging, lets data flow simultaneously into both, Application & Cloud Logging, and once you are satisfied, you can switch your observability procedures onto Cloud Logging and shutdown Application Logging.

It's complemented by a service innovation guide which helps to understand the full potential of Cloud Logging and where it differs from Application Logging.

1) Plan your Cloud Logging setup

Based on the knowledge from service Innovation guide, first plan the following:

  • How many instances?
  • How to centralize?
  • Size of each instance?
  • Who should have access to which instances?

2) Setup Identity Provider

In Cloud Logging, you have to configure your own user management (IDP). To have the same Single Sign On (SSO) experience, you must bring your own IAS account. Please refer to this guide on how to create a tenant SAML application in SAP BTP’S Identity Authentication Service (IAS).

  • SAML authentication cannot be enabled on an existing Cloud Logging Service instance with a service update. Parameters must be passed on service creation.
  • If you decide to utilize multiple CLS instances, you could use leverage the same SAML application from IAS to setup all CLS instances.

Note: Omitting the SAML configuration would fall back to Basic Authentication, which could be useful for evaluation purposes.

3) Setup Cloud Logging

Create instances of Cloud Logging Service and bind your applications based on our documentation.

  • Enter your SAML configuration as a configuration parameter derived from the previous step. You cannot enable SAML authentication on an existing Cloud Logging Service instance with a service update.
  • You have more freedom to configure, compared to Application Logging. For example, you can configure up to 90 days of retention. See Configuration Parameters to have a suitable parametrization.

4) Bind and instrument your applications

Bind your application to Cloud Logging – in parallel to the existing binding to Application Logging.

This let’s log data flow simultaneously into both, Application & Cloud Logging, so gives a smooth transition and allows to compare the results before the eventual switch.

Note: Since there is not ingestion-based quota in Cloud Logging, you might notice an increased ingestion as all logs are ingested. Application logging Statistics dashboard provides insights into the quota decisions. For each application, the aggregated message count and volume of accepted and rejected messages is reported for short time intervals. This allows determination of the total message volume, enabling an informed decision on the required service plan.

In case you use custom fields, please refer to the Annex B for instrumentation guide.

5) Validate Setup

Check if logs arrive and are parsed correctly. Remember, you have your own OpenSearch Dashboards, and your own URL which can be read from the cf service-key and that contains all endpoints and credentials.

To learn what you can do, check the using your Cloud Logging Service instance documentation and see how to ingest, alert, manage access rights, and so on.

6) Deprovision Application Logging

After at most 7 days, Cloud Logging instances hold all the logs which are in Application Logging.

So, you can unbind the applications from Application Logging instances (cf unbind-service) and delete the Application Logging service instance (cf delete-service). Existing bindings must be deleted before the instance can be deprovisioned.

 

Annex A) Process Visualization

The following visualizations explain the 6-step migration process via a concrete example on how the respective setups of application logging and cloud logging may look like.

 

Annex B) Adjust Custom Fields Configuration When Sending with Logging Libraries

This section applies only if you are sending custom fields with one of our logging libraries (Java/NodeJS). Application Logging requires configuring custom fields in the logging library, while Cloud Logging Service supports them without additional configuration.

The migration requires a configuration change to support both services simultaneously. You must send custom fields in #cf nested object and at top-level.

Node.js: The CF NodeJS logging library automatically detects which services you are bound to (Application Logging and/or Cloud Logging Service) and adjusts sending custom fields since version v6.5.0. Make sure you updated the logging library to this version or higher.

Java: You must configure the CF Java logging library, dependent on the logging backend you use.

  • For Log4j Logging Backend: Set retainOriginal to true in your log4j XML configuration file. That means replacing <customField mdcKeyName="custom-field" retainOriginal="false" /> with <customField mdcKeyName="custom-field" retainOriginal="true" /> for all custom fields.
  • For Logback Logging Backend: For each custom-field configuration line, another one has to be appended to retain that custom field at top level. For example, the <customFieldMdcKeyName>custom-field</customFieldMdcKeyName> configuration line should be followed by this one: <retainFieldMdcKeyName>custom-field</retainFieldMdcKeyName>.

After deprovisioning the Application Logging instance, you can remove all configuration regarding custom fields.

entry.title: Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance 

We are excited to announce the release of the new version of the Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance (MDG), which has recently been published on Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

This updated version includes new solution variants for SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA in classic mode and in cloud-ready mode. One of the key highlights of this release is the inclusion of detailed diagrams for business partner, product, and financial domain for master data governance in classic mode. These diagrams provide a comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, making it easier for users to understand how MDG fits into their existing IT landscape and how it can be integrated with other systems and applications.  

Updates in Detail

1. Solution Variant “Federated Master Data Governance with Core Data Owner on SAP S/4HANA”

We added the information that Federated Master Data Governance can also be used with MDG on lower releases/classic-mode. More information can be found in the how-to guide and in the blog "Consumption of Federated Governance Made Easy" from @Mark63.

In the next two solution variants, we have replaced the existing one for “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA” with two new solution variants to illustrate the supported processes: 

2. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Classic Mode”

In the Solution Value Flow Diagram, you can see that MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode supports multiple domains, including business partner, product, and financials. This diagram provides a clear representation of how a business process variant is implemented and supported by a specific solution. 

As example you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners. In the case of Decentral Creation, a consolidation process is used, whereas for Decentral Change, the MDG, Central Governance Change Request is used. 

The descriptive text next to the diagram contains more information: It provides details about the standard delivered process template and change request types that are suitable for decentralized creation and change processes.  

3. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Cloud-Ready Mode”

In comparison to the Solution Variant for MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode you can see the Solution Value Flow Diagram for cloud-ready mode currently only contains the Business Partner Domain. 

The Solution Process Flow Diagrams were initially published last year. We have now included three new diagrams, which demonstrate the support for Mass Load, Mass Change, and Mass Deletion of Business Partner Relationships. 

Furthermore, to draw a comparison with classic mode diagrams, here you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners in cloud-ready mode. In both cases, consolidation processes are used. 

We believe that this latest release will be a valuable resource for organizations looking to implement or expand their MDM capabilities. By providing a clear and comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, we aim to empower users to make the most of their MDM investment and to achieve their data management goals more efficiently.  

We invite you to explore the new Reference Solution Architecture for MDM by yourself on the Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

Further information

Curious about the initially released content and about the different diagram types such as Solution Value Flow diagram, Solution Component diagram, Solution Data Flow diagram, Solution Process Flow diagram? 

  • Check the blog post “Explore Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance” from my colleague @KarinBlank. 
  • Watch our Video: How to use Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance - YouTube 

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, join the Questions & Answers forum and Blog space, and check the MDG roadmap for all MDG versions. 

Best, Christina Lindner

entry.title: SAP Developer News, November 21st 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: SAP Developer News November 21st, 2024 - SAP Developers

DESCRIPTION

SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2411/ba-p/13940790

SAP TechEd On Tour from SAUG-x

  • Event Page: https://www.sap.com/australia/events/2024-11-19-au-pe-regional-conference-teched-on-tour-saugx.html

SAP BTP Innobytes October 2024 edition

  • Video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9xZCTtSGP_0

SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 and SAP Analytics Cloud 2024/Q4

  • SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 Released: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-hana-2-0-sps-08-released-safeguarding-customer-investments-and/ba-p/13942543
  • What’s New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 sessions: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-2-0-sps-08/ba-p/13941640
  • Calculation View Features of SAP HANA 2.0 SPS08: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/calculation-view-features-of-sap-hana-2-0-sps08/ba-p/13927860
  • What's New Viewer - SAP Analytics Cloud: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/42e4f84a0e5e458792b1047eaf81c31a?locale=en-US
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: Q4 2024 Release's Features with Experts & Demos: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=atNQxqkf0Z8&list=PL3ZRUb1AKkpTv44lv8pQvdlwT_MzAi3Qq&ab_channel=SAP

SAP Open Source Webinar on the OpenSearch Foundation

  • Details and sign-up https://events.sap.com/opensearch-foundation/en/registration.aspx

SAP Berlin Event

  • Info and to register: https://events.sap.com/eur/sapbuild-and-agilita-workshop/en/home

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:07 SAP TechEd On Tour from SAUG-x

1:04 SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

2:07 SAP BTP Innobytes October 2024 edition

3:19 SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 and SAP Analytics Cloud 2024/Q4

5:02 SAP Berlin Event

6:16 SAP Open Source Webinar on the OpenSearch Foundation

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for November 21st, 2024.

[Josh and Michelle] Hey everybody, this is Josh and Michelle, and we are live from Melbourne, Australia here at the SAUGx event part of the TechEd on tour. I had a great time being here not only taking part in keynote we are standing in the Developer Garage which you may recognize from other TechEd events. We did this in Florida at ASUG, we are doing here at SAUGx and I believe there's two more events coming up right Michelle? Yes, we have one in UKI and one in Germany a little bit later next year. The DSAG event is like in April, I think. Yeah. Yeah. So, we're on tour. We're coming after you guys. We hope that you enjoy meeting with us, the developer advocates in person. Lots of great things to cover from what was announced at TechEd, as well as lots of great tutorials you can complete for some great little prizes that we have here in the Developer Garage. Oh, absolutely. And they've been flying off as soon as people have been finishing so many tutorial tracks to get them all. Alright, so that's it from Australia. We'll see you guys later.

[Rich Heilman] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here coming to you live from SAUGx in Melbourne, Australia. I wanted to take a quick minute and let you know about the new release of SAP BTP ABAP environment 2411 and of course the new blog post from Nora Klemp of ABAP product management. Nora gives us all the details about the new features coming in release 2411 including items like, you can now use Multidimensional Analysis App Generator, Wizard, to create all ABAP development objects that are needed to build, expose, and deploy a Multidimensional Analysis Fiori application. So that's cool right? We're also getting some enhancements to BRF Plus and the XCO libraries, a ton of enhancements around business events, and much much more. For more information on the BTP ABAP environment released 24-11, check out Noor's blog post today. See you later. I'm gonna go jump on a plane. Bye for now, friends.

[DJ] Just in case you missed it, the October 2024 edition of SAP BTP InnoBytes is out. And it's all out AI and also Terraform. So on the AI front, there's a new SAP Business AI feature catalog on the SAP Discovery Center. And alongside the feature catalog, there's also a feature estimator with which you can work out costs and savings. And there's also a new generative AI based content generation assistant on the SAP Automation Pilot platform. And for setup of mission-related infrastructure on BTP, you can now use Terraform, which in contrast to the original booster-based setup mechanisms, allows you to download the Terraform scripts to use. And also, of course, that means you can adopt them and extend them for use in your own landscapes and environments. Open and free. Infrastructure as code. What's not to like? more about all these new features in this October 2024's edition of the SAP BTP InnoBytes video. Link in the description.

[Witalij] SAP HANA database powers over 70,000 customers worldwide, supporting some of the most critical applications and workloads. With the general availability of SAP HANA 2.0 SPS08 as of November 20, we at SAP are delivering key updates to enhance scalability, reliability, and developer experience while reducing the total cost of ownership. This release is designed to protect customer investments in our leading multi-model translitical database platform. Starting Friday, November 22, tune into SAP Developer's channel for our What's New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 series, hosted by truly yours, Developer Advocates. Over seven sessions, you'll hear from more than a dozen experts from the SAP HANA product management team. Each session will be packed with insights into the latest features and updates, along with guidance to help you make the most of them. Check out the full schedule in the blog post linked below. Don't miss out and join us. The QRC 2024 release of SAP Analytics Cloud went GA this week as well. You can explore the full list of all new features and enhancements in the What's New viewer on the SAP help portal. The SAP Analytics Cloud team has already released a video showcasing all the significant updates, complete with demos presented by product experts. Discover the highlights of the Q4 2024 release including bulk migration from classic stories to optimized experience, analytics catalog and grouping assets, story extensibility enhancements and so much more. Check it out today and start using these new features in in your tenant now.

[Daniel] I remember being invited to the first SAP Build Berlin event. It was called something like Burgers Beyond Budgets or something like that. Anyway, it was a great event. I got to talk a little bit. Everybody did the hands-on. And now there's a second event. Not that I was invited to it, but it's still going to be a great event. And it's combined with a Christmas market. So, there will be discussions about SAP Build, then a hands-on exercise, which I think I know what it's going to be. And I think so therefore it'll be very good. And then you'll go to a different place, and you'll have a Christmas market. The only thing I know about the Christmas market is that there will be glue wine, if I said that correctly. Not that I'm a big fan. The first time I had it was exactly 20 years ago. I was in Heidelberg where it was freezing cold, and they gave you glue wine. and the only good thing about it was that it was hot. Any event, I would go to this event in Berlin, even if you're far away, I would make your business to come November 28th. Speakers, hands-on, Christmas markets. Be a great time. Hope to see you there.

[DJ] There's a public webinar in early December, open to all and organized by the SAP Open-Source Program Office. It's on the open search foundation, how and why it was formed and why open search and open-source enterprise grade search and observability suite was moved into that foundation plus lots more from two great speakers. The webinar is on Thursday, the 5th of December. Follow the link in the description to find out more and how to sign up. See you there.

entry.title: Integration architecture guide: Updated version available now
entry.content:
I am happy to share the news that an updated version of the integration architecture guide has been released! Based on SAP Business Technology Platform offerings available today, this document provides you with detailed guidance on how to evolve your existing integration architecture. As before the integration guide is structured along the concepts of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, such as integration styles and integration use case patterns.

This blog post gives you an overview about the most important news delivered with this version of the guide.

Process Integration

This chapter has been updated to include recommendations regarding the latest capabilities of SAP Integration Suite such as the new Edge Integration Cell option, Data Space Integration and Advanced Event Mesh.

You can also find consolidated information about how to elevate toward SAP Integration Suite – be it from SAP Process Integration/Orchestration or from SAP API Management and/or SAP Cloud Integration on SAP BTP Neo cloud platform. In the first case, Edge Integration Cell plays an important role: Deployed in customer-controlled environments, it allows you to run your integration scenarios locally within your own environments, without going through the public internet. Migrated existing integrations flows can be redeployed to and run by the Edge Integration Cell.

 

Scenarios using Edge Integration Cell

Event-Driven Integration

Event-driven architecture (EDA) is a well-known approach for designing and building decoupled, hyper-connected software systems and it’s rap­idly gaining popularity in the world of enterprise integration due to its reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. This chapter describes the role and interplay of SAP EDA solutions such as SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Application Event Hub, further capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and technologies for event-enabling SAP applications.

Event-Driven Architecture in the Enterprise

Data Integration

The guide also describes the role of SAP Datasphere with regards to data integration options. SAP Datasphere enables a business data fabric architecture and supports integration use case pattern like data ingestion, virtual data access, and data transformation and orchestration.

Overview of SAP Datasphere

User Integration

There is also a major overhaul of this chapter to provide recommendations regarding the latest SAP solutions which enable a central entry point for users to business solutions. These are SAP Start, SAP Mobile Start, SAP Build Work Zone (standard and advanced edition) and SAP Task Center.

Cross-solutions entry point on SAP Business Technology Platform

Furthermore, the technology recommendation for the chatbot integration use case pattern of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology reflects now the latest innovations by introducing Joule: Joule is a generative AI-based assistant embedded in SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio that provides proactive, contextualized insights from various data sources.

New Content Format and Access Point

In addition to updating the content of the integration architecture guide we also changed its format: The guide is now available as documentation at SAP Help Portal and comes with benefits such as easier navigation and search, option to bookmark, subscribe and to provide feedback to the content.

The integration architecture guide is an integral part of SAP BTP Guidance Framework which is the central access point for architects, developers and administrators to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP.

Integration architecture guide at SAP BTP Guidance Framework

The integration guide belongs to the decision guides of the framework and is interconnected with other assets of SAP BTP Guidance Framework such as the documentation of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology and SAP BTP reference architectures (for integration).

Enjoy reading the new guide! It would also be great to get your feedback, for instance which integration topics should be addressed with the next update. If you have any suggestions feel free to leave them as comments to this blog post. Happy integrating 😊

entry.title: Try before you buy: How SAP is strategically minimizing the risk of technology adoption
entry.content:
How to try SAP BTP before buying it

Trying before you buy provides a flexible way for businesses to explore SAP BTP with minimal risk. Through this program, companies—particularly existing SAP customers—can access, test, and validate solutions and tools before making a financial commitment.

Both technical and business users can participate, with direct access to a role-specific experience designed to meet their unique business requirements. This approach lets organizations experience SAP BTP in ways that support diverse business goals, enable dynamic proof-of-concept environments, and lead to the adoption of flexible consumption models.

The offering’s structure includes three tiers of engagement, each catering to different exploration and adoption needs:

  • Trial access: Users can register with an e-mail address to demo SAP BTP for up to 90 days without further obligation. This option is ideal for those seeking a hands-on introduction to cloud solutions from SAP.
  • Free tier: Users can gain ongoing, limited access to resources in SAP BTP. This model lets businesses explore tools and capabilities in real scenarios without additional costs, making it suitable for customers using SAP software for the first time.
  • Premium subscription options: SAP offers pay-as-you-go and enterprise agreement subscriptions for organizations ready to deepen their commitment. The pay-as-you-go model is consumption based, while enterprise agreements provide contract-based access that delivers up to 30% more value through consolidated usage.

What are the benefits of trying before you buy

Trying SAP BTP before you buy delivers three critical benefits that make this program an ideal choice for organizations exploring new technology investments.

  1. Low-risk experimentation

One of the top advantages of trying SAP BTP before you buy is the ability for IT teams to develop proof-of-concept models and experiment with emerging technologies with reduced financial risk. This allows for more confident decision-making and strategic planning.

The resulting flexibility allows companies to avoid the costs and potential pitfalls of large-scale implementations by confirming that the investment delivers as expected early on. Organizations can fine-tune their technology stack by testing cloud solutions in real-life scenarios to support business goals better and drive meaningful outcomes.

  1. Scalability with cost benefits

SAP’s enterprise agreement subscription provides scalability with a clear cost advantage, offering up to 30% more value than the pay-as-you-go model.

This structured model is especially beneficial for organizations aiming to expand at a predictable, lower cost, as it consolidates usage across services and resources. Businesses can scale their use of SAP BTP as needed while maintaining budget predictability and maximizing the value of their cloud investments.

  1. Proven road map for future adoption

Trying before you buy presents a strategic opportunity for our customers to deepen their engagement with the range of solutions available in SAP BTP. With flexible scaling, testing, and exploration options, the program enables customers to harness the potential of our software ecosystem without heavy up-front investments.

This approach not only enhances connectivity within the SAP software landscape but also empowers organizations to innovate confidently, knowing they can leverage a comprehensive ecosystem at their own pace. 

Where getting the most value from the cloud begins

For organizations navigating the complexities of their digital transformation, trying SAP BTP before they buy is more than a gateway to innovation. It provides a strategic framework for mitigating risk with the best-fit solutions.

SAP’s commitment to facilitating a low-risk, high-reward entry to cloud adoption is invaluable for companies looking to stay competitive in an increasingly cloud-centric world. As businesses evolve, trying before you buy serves as a practical starting point for building a comprehensive, adaptable, and cost-efficient cloud strategy.

Learn more about how you can try SAP BTP before you buy.

entry.title: A New Era for SAP BusinessObjects: Embracing Change in a Cloud-Driven World
entry.content:
In recent years, the conversation around digital transformation has been dominated by one word—cloud. Businesses across industries are flocking to cloud solutions, drawn by promises of scalability, agility, and cost-efficiency. But in the midst of this cloud-first narrative, SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, continues to invest in its on-premise solutions, especially SAP BusinessObjects (BO). This decision raises questions: Why is SAP still prioritizing on-premise solutions? What is the long-term strategy for SAP Business Intelligence (BI)? And how will this impact SAP’s large, diverse customer base?

Let’s deep-dive into these key questions and understand the roadmap ahead for SAP BusinessObjects.

1. The Cloud Dominates the Conversation, But Is It the Only Path?

In today’s IT landscape, cloud computing is a major topic of discussion. Cloud analytics, in particular, has experienced rapid growth as organizations see the advantages of lower infrastructure costs, quick deployment, and flexible resource scaling. Solutions like SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) offer seamless integration with other cloud-native applications, providing powerful tools for real-time analysis, machine learning, and predictive insights.

Despite the strong interest in cloud solutions, not every enterprise is eager to move away from their on-premise systems. This highlights SAP's thoughtful and strategic choice to continue investing in its on-premise SAP BusinessObjects platform.

2. Why Is SAP Still Investing in On-Premise Solutions?

This balanced approach allows enterprises to modernize at their own pace while giving on-premise customers more time to plan their BI roadmap for transitioning to the cloud.

3. SAP’s Enterprise Strategy: Enabling Live Enterprise Reporting

SAP is dedicated to Enterprise Reporting, which is an important part of its overall strategy for SAP BusinessObjects. The aim is to offer "Live Enterprise Reporting" capabilities that provide real-time business insights to help organizations make better decisions. Many companies still depend on SAP BusinessObjects for their daily operations and reporting, using its powerful reporting engine for everything from financial and operational reports to strategic KPIs.

As businesses change, SAP's vision is to enable live data connectivity between on-premise and cloud systems, allowing for smooth reporting no matter where the data is located.

4. SAP BusinessObjects vs. SAP Analytics Cloud: A Case Study Snapshot

The SAP customer base is diverse, with different levels of cloud adoption. Here’s a simple comparison based on case studies from various industries:

SAP BusinessObjects Customers: SAP still has many customers who rely on SAP BusinessObjects for their everyday enterprise reporting. These organizations often have complex, legacy data environments and specific compliance requirements, primarily in industries like manufacturing, healthcare, and financial services, where long-term data storage and auditing are essential.

SAP Analytics Cloud Customers: The SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) has gained popularity in industries that are more cloud-friendly and agile, such as retail, technology, and consumer services. SAC customers enjoy real-time insights, AI-driven analytics, and a user-friendly interface that empowers non-technical users.

Both platforms serve different needs, and SAP’s hybrid strategy recognizes that the future of business intelligence will likely involve a mix of both solutions rather than a one-size-fits-all approach.

Way Forward -

SAP officially confirmed they would continue to invest in SAP BusinessObjects with a future release named SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025.

BI 2025 is the next SAP BusinessObjects release after BI 4.3 which will be available by the end of 2024. Customers will have the choice of staying on-premise or moving to the cloud, whether that be on SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) or the Private Cloud Edition (PCE).

This future release will only focus on the most-adopted tools which will include:

Web Intelligence

Single source .unx universes

Crystal Reports Classic

SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office

BI platform (includes the BI Launchpad)

SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 will thus provide on-premise customers more time to plan their BI roadmap to the cloud.

What will not be included in BI 2025?

However, while this is great news for SAP BusinessObjects customers, SAP will stop investing in certain components for this future release:

entry.title: Unleashing ABAP Potential: An introduction to Extension Libraries (XCO)
entry.content:
Introduction

In the evolving world of SAP development, the Extension Libraries (XCO) in ABAP stand as a significant innovation. Designed for consistency, simplicity, and future readiness, XCO enables developers to extend their ABAP capabilities effortlessly. Moreover, XCO ensures compatibility across diverse ABAP environments, including traditional On-Premise systems and the ABAP environment on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

In this blog, we’ll delve into the key features of XCO, its advantages, and practical implementation examples, equipping you to use this versatile library effectively.

What Are ABAP Extension Libraries (XCO)?

XCO is a library framework that encapsulates reusable functionality, providing a clean interface and abstraction for typical development tasks. It is part of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) but is versatile enough for other ABAP contexts. Key aspects include:

  • Consistency: Standardised APIs and coding patterns.
  • Cross-Compatibility: Works seamlessly in both On-Premise ABAP and ABAP environment on BTP.
  • Scalability: Supports modular and clean code architecture, promoting reusability.

Why Use XCO?

  1. Simplifies Development: Pre-built functionality reduces boilerplate coding and focuses on business logic.
  2. Ensures Adaptability: Designed to adapt to both traditional systems and cloud-native environments.
  3. Future-Proof: Aligns with SAP’s strategic direction of cloud readiness.

Practical Example: Creating a Custom Log Using XCO

Let’s create a simple log entry feature using XCO, applicable in both environments.

Setup: Include XCO Libraries

 

 

 

 

DATA(logger) = xco_log=>factory->create_new_log( 
   iv_object      = CONV #( 'OBJECT' ) " Place holders for actual values
   iv_subobject   = 'SUBOBJECT'
   iv_external_id = 'EXTERNAL_ID' ).

 

 

 

Log a Message

 

 

 

logger->add_message(
   is_message = VALUE #(
      msgty = 'E'
      msgid = 'MESSAGE'. " Place holder for message class ID
      msgno = '000'
      msgv1 = 'This'
      msgv2 = 'is a'
      msgv3 = 'log'
      msgv4 = 'message.' ) ).

 

 

 

Enhanced Logging
Add dynamic context information for better traceability:

 

 

 

logger->add_last_symsg(
      iv_problem_class   = if_xco_log_constants=>co_problem_class-medium
      iv_level_of_detail = if_xco_log_constants=>co_level_of_detail-four ).

 

 

 

Best Practices for Using XCO

  • Modularise Your Code: Leverage XCO for repetitive tasks like logging, error handling, and HTTP communication.
  • Follow RAP Guidelines: Combine XCO with RAP for clean and extensible application designs.
  • Version Management: Regularly update libraries to leverage new features and improvements.

Conclusion
